steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$g(4) = |22| = 22.$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. The value of $g\left(4\right)$ is the value of $g\left(x\right)$ when $x=4$. Substituting $4$ for $x$ in the given equation yields $g\left(4\right)=\left|4+18\right|$, which is equivalent to $g\left(4\right)=\left|22\right|$, or $g\left(4\right)=22$. Therefore, the value of $g\left(4\right)$ is $22$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This would be the value of $g\left(4\right)$ if function $g$ was defined by $g\left(x\right)=-\left|18\right|$, not $g\left(x\right)=\left|x+18\right|$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This would be the value of $g\left(4\right)$ if function $g$ was defined by $g\left(x\right)=-\left|x\right|$, not $g\left(x\right)=\left|x+18\right|$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This would be the value of $g\left(4\right)$ if function $g$ was defined by $g\left(x\right)=\left|-x+18\right|$, not $g\left(x\right)=\left|x+18\right|$.
